ops.title: sync: chain reader reports existing commit missing (stale pack snapshot?)
ops.description: Observed 2026-07-07 during cc-notes sync on cc-notes itself: 'sync origin: download attachments: referenced attachments: read refs/cc-notes/notes/aa89b7ef...: incomplete chain: commit 35ebc3fd... missing (shallow clone?)'. git cat-file -t 35ebc3fd => commit (object present BEFORE any new fetch). A fresh cc-notes sync process immediately after succeeded (rounds: 1). Transient, self-healing across processes — consistent with an in-process object-store snapshot going stale after the sync loop fetches new packs.

ops.text: root-cause why sync's chain read reported commit 35ebc3fd missing while git cat-file -t found it in the same repo moments later (suspect: go-git storer snapshot not refreshed after mid-run fetch)
fix the stale read or, at minimum, make the error text stop suggesting 'shallow clone?' when the object exists locally
